key_concepts: A property boundary is the legally recognized line dividing two parcels.
A survey is a professional measurement of land boundaries and structures.
A plat map is an official map showing lot lines, dimensions, and recorded boundaries.
Encroachment refers to a structure crossing onto another parcel.
Long-standing structures may appear on older surveys or plats.
County records often contain historical plats, deeds, and surveys.
typical_steps: People often start by gathering their deed, survey, and plat map.
They compare historical and current surveys to understand changes.
They sometimes check county GIS maps for parcel boundaries.
They may consult a licensed surveyor for clarification.
They often document the history of the structure and its placement.
They may review any easements or right-of-way records.
notes: Boundary disputes often involve comparing multiple surveys.
Long-standing structures may appear on older plats or aerial imagery.
County offices typically maintain historical land records.
